description: Replaced the Indian Penal Code 1860 as India's primary criminal law, effective July 2024. Covers offences and punishments.
key_provisions: Sections 63-99: Offences against women (rape, trafficking, harassment)|Sections 100-146: Offences against state and public peace|Sections 303-334: Property offences (theft, dacoity)|Section 3(5): Organised crime and terrorism|Replaces IPC 1860

description: Replaced the Code of Criminal Procedure 1973; governs criminal trials, investigations, bail, and arrests, effective July 2024.
key_provisions: Section 35: Rights of arrested persons; must be produced before magistrate within 24 hours|Section 173: FIR registration mandatory for cognizable offences|Sections 478-496: Bail provisions|Section 530: Summary trials for petty offences|45-day timeline for completing investigation

description: Replaced the Indian Evidence Act 1872; governs admissibility and evaluation of evidence in Indian courts.
key_provisions: Section 24: Confessions to police not admissible|Section 57: Electronic records admissible|Section 111: Burden of proof shifts in certain cases|Explicitly recognises digital and electronic evidence

description: The principal criminal code of India covering offences and punishments; superseded by Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita 2023 from July 2024.
key_provisions: Section 302: Murder (death or life imprisonment)|Section 376: Rape|Section 378-420: Theft, cheating, fraud|Section 499-500: Defamation|Section 304B: Dowry death

description: Procedural law governing criminal trials and investigations; superseded by BNSS 2023 from July 2024.
key_provisions: Section 154: FIR registration|Section 41: Arrest without warrant|Section 167: Remand to custody|Section 125: Maintenance for wives, children, parents|Section 438: Anticipatory bail

description: Governed admissibility of evidence in Indian courts; superseded by Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam 2023.
key_provisions: Section 3: Definition of evidence|Section 24: Confessions to police inadmissible|Section 65B: Admissibility of electronic records|Section 112: Presumption of legitimacy

description: Punishes corruption by public servants; 2018 amendment also covers bribe givers.
key_provisions: Section 7: Offence of bribery (3-7 years)|Section 13: Criminal misconduct by public servant|Section 17A: Prior sanction required to prosecute senior officials|2018 Amendment: Bribe givers also liable
enforcing_authority: Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI), State Anti-Corruption Bureaus

description: Combats money laundering and provides for attachment and confiscation of proceeds of crime.
key_provisions: Section 3: Offence of money laundering|Section 8: Attachment of property|Section 17: Search and seizure by Enforcement Directorate|Section 45: Strict bail conditions in money laundering cases

description: Prevents and punishes unlawful activities and terrorist acts; provides for designation of terrorist organisations.
key_provisions: Section 10: Membership of unlawful association|Section 15: Terrorist act (up to death penalty)|Section 35: Power to designate terrorist organisations|Section 43D: Stringent bail conditions

act_name: Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, 1985
description: Controls operations related to narcotic drugs and psychotropic substances; imposes strict penalties for drug trafficking.
key_provisions: Section 20: Cannabis offences|Section 21: Opium offences|Section 22: Psychotropic substances|Small quantity: up to 1 year|Commercial quantity: 10-20 years and Rs.1-2 lakh fine|Section 37: Bail restrictions for commercial quantity

description: The supreme law of India; establishes government framework, fundamental rights, and directive principles.
key_provisions: Part III (Articles 12-35): Fundamental Rights (equality, freedom, right to life)|Part IV: Directive Principles|Article 32: Right to approach Supreme Court for fundamental rights|Article 226: Writ jurisdiction of High Courts
enforcing_authority: Supreme Court of India, High Courts

description: Governs acquisition, determination, and termination of Indian citizenship.
key_provisions: Section 3: Citizenship by birth|Section 5: Citizenship by registration|Section 6: Citizenship by naturalisation (11 years residency)|CAA 2019: Fast-track citizenship for persecuted minorities from 3 neighbouring countries

description: Regulates elections to Parliament and State Legislatures including voter rolls, candidates, and election offences.
key_provisions: Section 8: Disqualification on conviction for specified offences|Section 77: Limit on election expenses|Section 123: Corrupt practices (bribery, undue influence)|Section 125A: Penalty for filing false declaration

description: Governs formation, performance, and enforcement of contracts in India — the foundation of commercial law.
key_provisions: Section 2: Definitions of offer, acceptance, consideration|Section 10: What agreements are contracts|Section 23: Void agreements (unlawful consideration)|Section 73: Compensation for breach of contract|Sections 148-181: Bailment and pledge

description: Governs the procedure for filing and conducting civil suits in Indian courts.
key_provisions: Order 7: Plaint (statement of claim)|Order 8: Written statement by defendant|Order 39: Temporary injunctions|Order 21: Execution of decree|Section 80: Mandatory notice before suing government

description: Provides for specific enforcement of contracts and prevention of breach of obligations. 2018 amendment made enforcement a right, not discretion.
key_provisions: Section 10: Specific performance of contracts (mandatory since 2018 amendment)|Section 31: Cancellation of instruments|Section 38: Perpetual injunction|2018 Amendment: Infrastructure contracts must be specifically enforced

description: Prescribes time limits for filing legal suits; suits filed after the limitation period are barred.
key_provisions: Article 54: 3 years for suit for specific performance|Article 65: 12 years for recovery of immovable property|Section 5: Court may condone delay for sufficient cause|Section 18: Effect of fraud on limitation period

description: Governs transfer of immovable property between living persons through sale, mortgage, lease, and gift.
key_provisions: Section 5: Transfer of property defined|Section 54: Sale of immovable property|Section 58: Mortgage (6 types)|Section 105: Lease of immovable property|Section 123: Gift of property

description: Mandates registration of documents relating to immovable property and other specified documents.
key_provisions: Section 17: Compulsory registration (sale deeds, mortgages, leases over 1 year)|Section 18: Optional registration|Section 49: Unregistered documents inadmissible in court|Section 32: Who may present documents for registration

description: Governs negotiable instruments like cheques, bills of exchange, and promissory notes. Section 138 covers cheque bounce.
key_provisions: Section 6: Cheque defined|Section 138: Dishonour of cheque — criminal liability; up to 2 years or fine up to twice the cheque amount|Section 139: Presumption in favour of holder|Section 142: Complaint must be filed within 30 days

description: Governs marriages among Hindus, Buddhists, Jains, and Sikhs; provides for divorce, judicial separation, and maintenance.
key_provisions: Section 5: Conditions for valid Hindu marriage|Section 13: Grounds for divorce (adultery, cruelty, desertion 2+ years, mental disorder)|Section 24: Maintenance pendente lite|Section 9: Restitution of conjugal rights|Section 26: Custody of children

description: Governs intestate succession among Hindus. 2005 amendment gave daughters equal rights in HUF (coparcenary) property.
key_provisions: Section 8: General rules of succession for Hindu males|Section 14: Hindu female's property (full ownership)|2005 Amendment — Section 6: Daughters equal coparceners in HUF property|Section 15: Succession for Hindu females

description: Governs adoption among Hindus and maintenance obligations within Hindu families.
key_provisions: Sections 7-8: Who may adopt (any Hindu male/female of sound mind)|Section 10: Person who may be adopted (Hindu, generally under 15)|Section 18: Maintenance of wife|Section 20: Maintenance of children and aged parents

act_name: Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act, 2005
description: Protects women against all forms of domestic violence — physical, emotional, sexual, verbal, and economic abuse.
key_provisions: Section 12: Application for protection order|Section 18: Protection order against abuser|Section 19: Residence order (woman cannot be evicted)|Section 20: Monetary relief|Section 21: Custody order for children

description: Prohibits giving, taking, or demanding dowry in connection with a marriage.
key_provisions: Section 3: Penalty for giving/taking dowry — 5 years imprisonment and fine|Section 4: Penalty for demanding dowry|Section 4A: Advertisements for dowry prohibited|Section 7: Cognizance by first class magistrate

description: Prohibits child marriages; boys below 21 and girls below 18 cannot be legally married.
key_provisions: Section 3: Child marriage voidable at minor's option|Section 9: Penalty for adult male marrying a child|Section 10: Penalty for solemnising child marriage|Section 13: Injunction to prevent child marriage|Section 14: Child Marriage Prohibition Officers in each district
enforcing_authority: Child Marriage Prohibition Officers, Police, Magistrates

description: Provides for civil marriages between persons of any religion, caste, or nationality in India.
key_provisions: Section 4: Conditions for marriage|Section 5: 30-day notice of intended marriage|Section 11: Certificate of marriage issued|Section 27: Grounds for divorce (adultery, cruelty, desertion)|Sections 36-37: Maintenance provisions

act_name: Muslim Women (Protection of Rights on Marriage) Act, 2019
description: Declares instant triple talaq (talaq-e-biddat) void and illegal; makes it a criminal offence punishable with 3 years imprisonment.
key_provisions: Section 3: Triple talaq declared void and illegal|Section 4: Punishment — 3 years imprisonment and fine|Section 7: Cognizance only on complaint by wife or blood relative|Section 5: Entitlement to subsistence allowance and custody of children

description: Mandates minimum wages for workers in scheduled employments to prevent exploitation.
key_provisions: Section 3: Government power to fix minimum wages|Section 12: Employer obligation to pay minimum wages|Section 20: Claims for underpayment to Labour Commissioner|Section 22: Penalties — 6 months jail or fine

description: Ensures regular and timely payment of wages to workers and limits permissible deductions from wages.
key_provisions: Section 4: Monthly wage periods|Section 5: Payment by 7th or 10th of the month|Section 7: Permissible deductions|Section 15: Claims for unpaid wages to Labour Authority

description: Governs investigation and settlement of industrial disputes; covers strikes, lockouts, and retrenchment.
key_provisions: Section 2(k): Industrial dispute defined|Section 25F: Retrenchment requires 1 month notice and compensation|Section 25N: Prior government permission for layoffs in large establishments|Section 10: Reference of disputes to adjudication|Section 22: Prohibition of strikes and lockouts
enforcing_authority: Labour Commissioner, Industrial Tribunals, Labour Courts

act_name: Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952
description: Provides compulsory provident fund, pension, and deposit-linked insurance for employees in establishments with 20+ workers.
key_provisions: Employer and employee each contribute 12% of basic wages to EPF|Employee Pension Scheme (EPS) provides monthly pension after retirement|Employees eligible for withdrawal on resignation, retirement, illness|Section 14B: Penalties for delayed deposits

description: Provides medical and cash benefits to workers earning up to Rs.21,000/month and their dependants.
key_provisions: Employer contributes 3.25% and employee contributes 0.75% of wages|Medical benefits for insured person and family|Cash benefits during sickness, maternity, and disability|Applicable to establishments with 10+ employees

description: Regulates working conditions in factories to ensure safety, health, and welfare of workers.
key_provisions: Section 51: 9 hours per day; 48 hours per week maximum|Section 79: Annual leave with wages|Section 6: Approval, licensing and registration of factories|Sections 22-24: Safety provisions for machinery|Section 41B: Compulsory disclosure for hazardous processes
enforcing_authority: Chief Inspector of Factories, State Labour Department

description: Provides maternity leave and benefits to women employees; 2017 amendment extended leave to 26 weeks.
key_provisions: Section 5: 26 weeks paid maternity leave (first two children); 12 weeks for third child|Section 9A: Crèche mandatory for establishments with 50+ employees|Section 11A: Work from home option after maternity leave|Applies to establishments with 10+ employees

description: Mandates payment of gratuity to employees on retirement, resignation, death, or disablement after 5 years of continuous service.
key_provisions: Section 4: Gratuity at 15 days wages per year of service|Maximum gratuity capped at Rs.20 lakh|Nominee receives gratuity on employee death even before 5 years|Applicable to establishments with 10+ employees

act_name: Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (Prevention, Prohibition and Redressal) Act, 2013
description: Protects women from sexual harassment at workplace; requires every organisation with 10+ employees to form an Internal Complaints Committee (ICC).
key_provisions: Section 4: ICC mandatory for 10+ employee organisations|Section 6: Local Complaints Committee (LCC) for informal sector|Section 9: Complaint within 3 months (extendable to 6 months)|Section 26: Rs.50,000 fine for non-compliance|Section 16: Conciliation possible at complainant's request
enforcing_authority: Internal Complaints Committee (ICC), Local Complaints Committee, District Officer

description: Mandates equal pay to men and women workers for the same or similar work; prohibits gender-based discrimination in recruitment.
key_provisions: Section 4: Duty of employer to pay equal remuneration|Section 5: No gender discrimination in recruitment|Section 8: Employer to maintain registers|Section 10: Penalties for non-compliance

description: Abolished the bonded labour system in India; debt bondage agreements are void and bonded labourers are freed from all obligations.
key_provisions: Section 4: Bonded labour system abolished|Section 5: All bonded debts extinguished|Section 6: No creditor to enforce bonded debt|Section 16: Penalty for enforcing bonded labour (3 years jail and fine)|Section 14: Vigilance Committees to identify and rehabilitate bonded labourers

description: Protects consumers against defective goods, deficient services, and unfair trade practices; established CCPA and e-commerce protections.
key_provisions: Section 2(7): Consumer defined (includes online purchases)|Section 18: Central Consumer Protection Authority (CCPA)|Sections 28-58: Consumer Disputes Redressal Commissions at district, state, and national level|Section 86: Product liability for manufacturer, seller, and service provider|E-Commerce Rules 2020: Platforms must have grievance officer

description: Promotes fair competition and prevents anti-competitive agreements, abuse of dominant position, and regulates mergers.
key_provisions: Section 3: Anti-competitive agreements prohibited (cartels, price-fixing)|Section 4: Abuse of dominant position prohibited|Sections 5-6: Regulation of combinations (mergers and acquisitions)|CCI can impose penalties up to 10% of average annual turnover

description: Governs incorporation, regulation, and dissolution of companies in India with major corporate governance reforms.
key_provisions: Section 7: Incorporation of companies|Section 135: CSR mandatory for qualifying companies|Section 149: Independent directors requirement|Section 166: Duties of directors|Section 245: Class action suits by shareholders|Section 447: Fraud — 10 years imprisonment
enforcing_authority: Ministry of Corporate Affairs, Registrar of Companies, NCLT

description: Provides for time-bound insolvency resolution of companies and individuals; protects creditors' rights.
key_provisions: Section 7: Financial creditors can initiate CIRP|Section 9: Operational creditors can initiate CIRP (default Rs.1 crore+)|Section 12: 180 days (extendable to 270 days) for resolution|Section 53: Waterfall mechanism for distribution of proceeds

description: Protects homebuyers from fraudulent real estate developers; mandates registration of projects and agents with State RERA.
key_provisions: Section 3: Mandatory registration of real estate projects|Section 11: Developer obligations (timely delivery, no fund diversion)|Section 18: Refund with interest if developer delays possession|Section 19: Buyer rights to know project status|Section 31: Complaint to State RERA Authority

description: Governs formation, operation, and dissolution of partnerships in India.
key_provisions: Section 4: Partnership defined|Section 11: Rights of partners|Section 13: Mutual rights and duties of partners|Section 32: Expulsion of partner|Section 44: Grounds for dissolution of firm

description: Framework environmental law enabling the central government to protect and improve environmental quality.
key_provisions: Section 3: Powers of central government for environmental protection|Section 7: Prohibition on discharge of pollutants above prescribed limits|Section 15: Penalties — 5 years jail and Rs.1 lakh fine|EIA Notification 2006: Mandatory environmental impact assessment for major projects
enforcing_authority: Ministry of Environment Forest and Climate Change (MoEFCC), CPCB

act_name: Water (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1974
description: Prevents and controls water pollution; established Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) and State PCBs.
key_provisions: Section 24: Prohibition on using streams for polluting disposal|Section 25: Consent of State Board required for effluent discharge|Section 43: Penalties for non-compliance|Established CPCB and State Pollution Control Boards
enforcing_authority: Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB), State PCBs

act_name: Air (Prevention and Control of Pollution) Act, 1981
description: Prevents, controls and abates air pollution; requires consent from State Pollution Control Board for polluting industries.
key_provisions: Section 21: Consent of State Board required to operate industrial plant|Section 31A: Power to give directions|Section 37: Penalties for violating provisions
enforcing_authority: Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB), State PCBs

description: Provides for protection of wild animals, birds, and plants; created the framework for wildlife sanctuaries and national parks.
key_provisions: Schedules I-VI: Degrees of protection for different species|Section 9: Hunting of scheduled animals prohibited|Section 27: Entry into sanctuaries regulated|Section 51: Penalties — 3-7 years imprisonment
enforcing_authority: Ministry of Environment Forest and Climate Change, Wildlife Crime Control Bureau

act_name: Scheduled Tribes and Other Traditional Forest Dwellers (Recognition of Forest Rights) Act, 2006
description: Recognises and vests forest rights in forest-dwelling Scheduled Tribes and other traditional forest dwellers.
key_provisions: Section 3: Forest rights recognised (individual and community)|Right to reside in and cultivate forest land up to 4 hectares|Community rights over forest resources, water, and biodiversity|Section 6: Gram Sabha to determine rights|Section 4: No eviction without recognition of rights
enforcing_authority: Gram Sabha, Sub-Divisional Level Committee, State-Level Monitoring Committee

description: Provides legal recognition for electronic transactions and defines cyber crimes and their punishments.
key_provisions: Section 43: Penalty for damage to computer systems|Section 66: Hacking — 3 years jail and Rs.5 lakh fine|Section 66C: Identity theft — 3 years imprisonment|Section 66D: Cheating by impersonation using computer|Section 67: Publishing obscene material online|Section 69: Government power to intercept communications
enforcing_authority: Cyber Crime Cells, Ministry of Electronics and IT (MeitY)

description: India's comprehensive data protection law governing collection, storage, and processing of personal data by data fiduciaries.
key_provisions: Section 4: Processing of personal data only for lawful purpose with consent|Section 6: Informed, specific, freely given consent required|Section 8: Rights of data principals (access, correction, erasure, grievance)|Section 9: Restrictions on processing children's data|Section 33-34: Data Protection Board to adjudicate complaints|Penalties up to Rs.250 crore for major violations
enforcing_authority: Data Protection Board of India, Ministry of Electronics and IT

act_name: Aadhaar (Targeted Delivery of Financial and Other Subsidies, Benefits and Services) Act, 2016
description: Provides statutory backing to the Aadhaar unique identification system; governs use of Aadhaar for delivering government benefits.
key_provisions: Section 3: Every resident entitled to Aadhaar number|Section 29: Aadhaar data cannot be shared without consent|Section 33: Disclosure only on court order|Aadhaar cannot be mandatory for private services (SC ruling 2018)|Section 47: Offences and penalties for misuse

description: Provides for mental healthcare and services; establishes rights of persons with mental illness and reduces stigma.
key_provisions: Section 3: Right to access mental health care|Section 18: Right to community living|Section 20: Right to equality and non-discrimination|Section 115: Attempt to suicide presumed under severe stress; no punishment under BNS|Advance directive allowed for future treatment decisions
enforcing_authority: State Mental Health Authority, Mental Health Review Boards

description: Regulates import, manufacture, sale, and distribution of drugs and cosmetics to ensure safety and efficacy.
key_provisions: Section 18: Prohibition on manufacture/sale of spurious drugs|Schedule H/H1: Prescription-only drugs|Schedule X: Narcotic and psychotropic drugs (strict control)|Section 27: Penalties for violations — 2-7 years for adulterated drugs
enforcing_authority: Central Drugs Standard Control Organisation (CDSCO), State Drug Controllers

description: Empowers governments to take special measures to prevent spread of dangerous epidemic diseases.
key_provisions: Section 2: State government can prescribe regulations for epidemic control|Section 2A: Central government can inspect vessels and detain persons|2020 Ordinance: Attacks on healthcare workers are cognizable offence|Used extensively during COVID-19 pandemic
enforcing_authority: State Governments, Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

description: Empowers every citizen to request information from public authorities; response must be given within 30 days.
key_provisions: Section 3: Every citizen has right to information|Section 7: Response within 30 days (48 hours if life or liberty at stake)|Section 6: RTI application fee Rs.10 (BPL cardholders exempt)|Section 19: First and second appeals to Central/State Information Commission|Section 20: Penalty Rs.250/day up to Rs.25,000 for non-compliance
enforcing_authority: Public Information Officers, Central/State Information Commissions

description: Provides free legal services to eligible persons and establishes Lok Adalats for speedy dispute resolution.
key_provisions: Section 12: Eligible persons (women, children, SC/ST, workers, disabled, income below Rs.3 lakh)|Section 19: Lok Adalats for pre-litigation and pending cases|Section 21: Lok Adalat award is deemed decree of civil court|Section 22B: Permanent Lok Adalats for public utility services
enforcing_authority: NALSA, State Legal Services Authorities (SLSA), District Legal Services Authorities (DLSA)

act_name: Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989
description: Prevents atrocities against Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes; provides for special courts and victim relief.
key_provisions: Section 3: 19 atrocity offences (forced labour, social boycott, land dispossession)|2018 Amendment: 6 more offences added|Section 14: Exclusive Special Courts for trials|No anticipatory bail for accused|Sections 15A-15B: Rights of victims and witnesses

description: Prohibits employment of children below 14 in all occupations; adolescents 14-18 prohibited from hazardous work.
key_provisions: Section 3: Children below 14 cannot be employed in any occupation|Section 3A: Adolescents (14-18) prohibited from hazardous work|Section 14: Penalties — 6 months to 2 years and fine|2016 Amendment: Tightened provisions; family enterprise with conditions allowed

act_name: Protection of Children from Sexual Offences Act, 2012
description: Protects children below 18 from sexual abuse, harassment, and exploitation; provides for child-friendly court procedures.
key_provisions: Section 4: Penetrative sexual assault — 7 years to life imprisonment|Section 7-8: Sexual assault on child — 3-5 years|Section 11-12: Sexual harassment of child|Section 19: Mandatory reporting; failure to report is an offence|Section 23: Court shall not disclose identity of child victim|Section 28: Special Courts for speedy trial

act_name: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016
description: Recognises 21 types of disabilities; mandates 4% reservation in government jobs and free education for disabled children 6-18.
key_provisions: Section 2: 21 types of disabilities recognised (autism, cerebral palsy, Parkinson's, dwarfism, etc.)|Section 32: 4% reservation in government establishments|Section 31: Free and inclusive education for disabled children 6-18|Section 16: Government to ensure access to courts, police stations, hospitals|Chief Commissioner and State Commissioners for grievance redressal
enforcing_authority: Chief Commissioner for Persons with Disabilities, State Commissioners

act_name: Maintenance and Welfare of Parents and Senior Citizens Act, 2007
description: Requires children/relatives to maintain parents and senior citizens; provides for Maintenance Tribunals and old age homes.
key_provisions: Section 4: Children obligated to maintain parents|Section 5: Application to Maintenance Tribunal (SDM)|Section 9: Tribunal to pass order within 90 days|Section 10: Maximum maintenance up to Rs.10,000/month|Section 23: Property transfer void if relative fails to provide maintenance

description: Provides subsidised food grains to about two-thirds of India's population under the Public Distribution System (PDS).
key_provisions: Priority households entitled to 5 kg food grains per person per month at Rs.1-3/kg|Antyodaya households entitled to 35 kg per month|Section 12: District Grievance Redressal Officer to be appointed|Pregnant women entitled to maternity benefit of Rs.6,000|Children 6 months to 6 years entitled to free meals under ICDS
enforcing_authority: Ministry of Consumer Affairs, State Food Corporations

act_name: Right of Children to Free and Compulsory Education Act, 2009
description: Makes free and compulsory education a fundamental right for children aged 6-14 under Article 21A of the Constitution.
key_provisions: Section 3: Free and compulsory education for children 6-14|Section 12(1)(c): Private schools must reserve 25% seats for economically weaker sections|Section 16: No detention/expulsion until Class 8|Section 17: Physical punishment and mental harassment prohibited|Section 23: Teacher qualifications and training requirements
enforcing_authority: Ministry of Education, State Education Departments

description: Guarantees 100 days of wage employment per year to rural households whose adult members volunteer for unskilled manual work.
key_provisions: 100 days of guaranteed employment per household per year|Employment must be provided within 15 days of demand or unemployment allowance paid|Minimum wages applicable; wages paid within 15 days of work|Works to be within 5 km of residence|Social audit mandatory
enforcing_authority: Ministry of Rural Development, Gram Panchayats

description: Established the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) and State Human Rights Commissions to protect human rights.

enforcing_authority: National Human Rights Commission (NHRC), State Human Rights Commissions

act_name: Right to Fair Compensation and Transparency in Land Acquisition, Rehabilitation and Resettlement Act, 2013
description: Regulates government acquisition of private land; mandates fair compensation and comprehensive rehabilitation for affected families.
key_provisions: Social Impact Assessment mandatory before acquisition|70-80% consent of affected families required for private projects|Compensation: 4x market value in rural; 2x in urban areas|Comprehensive rehabilitation and resettlement package|No multi-crop irrigated land acquisition without necessity

description: Established the Lokpal at the central level and mandated Lokayuktas in states for investigating corruption complaints against public functionaries.
key_provisions: Section 15: Lokpal can investigate PM, Union Ministers, MPs, Group A-D officials|Section 16: Investigation to be completed within 6 months|Section 23: CBI under Lokpal for corruption cases|Section 60: No sanction required for prosecution in Lokpal cases

description: Protects persons making public interest disclosures regarding corruption or wilful misuse of power by public servants.

enforcing_authority: Central Vigilance Commission (CVC), State Vigilance Commissions

act_name: Protection of Plant Varieties and Farmers' Rights Act, 2001
description: Protects plant varieties and farmers' rights; establishes Plant Varieties Protection and Farmers' Rights Authority.
key_provisions: Section 39: Farmers' rights to save, use, sow, exchange and sell farm produce|Section 41: Registration of new plant varieties|Section 28: Breeder rights on registration (15-18 years protection)|Section 64: Compensation to farmers for non-performance of variety
enforcing_authority: Plant Varieties Protection and Farmers' Rights Authority

description: Established the University Grants Commission to coordinate, determine, and maintain standards of higher education in India.
key_provisions: Section 3: UGC established as statutory body|Section 22: Only UGC-approved institutions can award degrees|Section 25: UGC can withhold grants for non-compliance|UGC Regulations on Minimum Standards for Teachers|UGC Anti-Ragging Regulations
